analysis_summary: The proposed method produced 4 finding(s) from 6 analysis obligation(s).

findings.summary: Debug master can access any peripheral with permissions in its access_ctrl row, using the processor's current privilege level, potentially bypassing intended restrictions.
findings.vulnerability_category: read or write / any access-controlled slave (ROM2, AES, UART, etc.) on master[0:11] / permission-related RTL security issue

evidence.description: no HW auth for JTAG enable
single priv_lvl_i used for all slave indices

findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: debug master (i_axi_master_dm connected to slave[1]) via JTAG/DMI path Operation: read or write Object: any access-controlled slave (ROM2, AES, UART, etc.) on master[0:11] Expected guard: Independent debug priv_lvl or debug authentication gating access_ctrl evaluation for slave[1] Observed behavior: Debug master shares processor's priv_lvl signal; its access_ctrl row (index 1) is evaluated using priv_lvl_processor. No separate debug authorization. Path: jtag_enable → i_SimJTAG → i_dmi_jtag → jtag_dmi_req → debug MUX → i_dm_top → dm_axi_m_req → i_axi_master_dm → slave[1] → i_axi_xbar → connectivity_map(slave=1, priv_lvl=processor) → master[j] Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Debug master can access any peripheral whose access_ctrl_reg[1][*][priv_lvl_processor] bit is set; if access_ctrl_reg[1] is permissive, debug can read/write all slaves including ROM2 keys, AES, UART

findings.summary: access_ctrl_reg implementation inside ariane_peripherals module is not available in the visible RTL; write gating, privilege check, and lock mechanisms cannot be inspected.
findings.vulnerability_category: write / access_ctrl_reg (access control configuration) / permission-related RTL security issue

evidence.description: access_ctrl derived from access_ctrl_reg via bit slicing
ROM2 mem contains initial values for access control master 0 and 1, but loading path into access_ctrl_reg is in the inaccessible ariane_peripherals

findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: any bus master that can write to access_ctrl_reg address Operation: write Object: access_ctrl_reg (access control configuration) Expected guard: Machine-mode only write access with optional lock bit Observed behavior: access_ctrl_reg implementation inside ariane_peripherals module is not available in the visible RTL; write gating, privilege check, and lock mechanisms cannot be inspected. Path: Unknown – ariane_peripherals source file missing Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: If access_ctrl_reg is writable by a lower-privilege master and lacks a lock, an attacker could escalate privileges to access any peripheral.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: ariane_peripherals source not available; cannot inspect write gating, privilege check, or lock mechanism.

findings.summary: PLIC access granted via CLINT permission bit creates unintended sharing of permissions.
findings.vulnerability_category: access (read/write) the PLIC peripheral / PLIC (interrupt controller) / permission-related RTL security issue

evidence.description: s_connectivity_map passed as cfg_connectivity_map_i to axi_node

findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: any master with CLINT permission but no PLIC permission Operation: access (read/write) the PLIC peripheral Object: PLIC (interrupt controller) Expected guard: PLIC access controlled solely by its own permission bit, not derived from CLINT Observed behavior: connectivity_map_o[i][6] = access_ctrl_i[i][6][priv_lvl_i] || access_ctrl_i[i][7][priv_lvl_i]; a master with CLINT access (i.e., j=7 bit set) gains PLIC access regardless of the dedicated PLIC bit. Path: connectivity_mapping module (generate block) → s_connectivity_map → cfg_connectivity_map_i → axi_node → master[6] (PLIC) Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: A master intended to have only CLINT access can read/write PLIC, potentially manipulating interrupt priorities, enabling/disabling interrupts, and interfering with the security model.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Unclear if PLIC/CLINT sharing is intentional; requires system security policy confirmation.

findings.summary: ndmreset is generated by dm_top and connected to AXI node reset (ndmreset_n) and to ariane_peripherals (ndmreset_n). The effect on access_ctrl_reg cannot be verified because ariane_peripherals source is missing.
findings.vulnerability_category: reset / access_ctrl_reg or any lock/security state registers / permission-related RTL security issue

evidence.description: access_ctrl_reg feeds access_ctrl, but reg implementation location unknown

findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: ndmreset (debug non-maskable reset) Operation: reset Object: access_ctrl_reg or any lock/security state registers Expected guard: ndmreset should not reset security configuration, or assertion protected by authentication Observed behavior: ndmreset is generated by dm_top and connected to AXI node reset (ndmreset_n) and to ariane_peripherals (ndmreset_n). The effect on access_ctrl_reg cannot be verified because ariane_peripherals source is missing. Path: dm_top → ndmreset → rstgen (ndmreset_n) → ariane_peripherals.rst_ni; access_ctrl_reg flip-flop reset condition unknown Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: If ndmreset resets access_ctrl_reg to a permissive default, an attacker via debug could clear all access restrictions and gain unlimited peripheral access.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: access_ctrl_reg implementation inside ariane_peripherals not available; cannot verify if ndmreset resets access_ctrl_reg or its reset value.

global_uncertainty: No closure-level missing evidence was recorded beyond the visible source scope limitations.
